title: Double-Sided 5x7 THC Beverage Postcard Design

evidence: image 1 (distributor_logo): Arch & Prairie Beverage Co. logo matches named brand exactly
images 2-10 cover all 9 named brands (HiSide, Howdy, THC Social, Stay Cool, SUP, 8th Wonder, Tempters, CURRNT, Don't Be That Dude)
deductions: No branded product shots (actual THC beverage cans/bottles); lifestyle photos show generic unlabeled cans
Brief says 'product photos will be provided' — generic stock substituted, reducing brand specificity
'Howdy' logo (image 3) has a western rope style, slightly less 'modern premium'.
'Tempters' logo (image 8) script font and devil tail are less 'premium hospitality'.
All other assets align well with the 'clean, modern, premium' brief.
brand_logos[2] and brand_logos[3] are visually weak/low-res for premium brief

evidence: postcard_copy.json contains all front + back copy, all 9 brand names, compliance line, both QR labels and URLs
images 14-15 (qr_codes[0], qr_codes[1]): two distinct QR codes present and scannable-format
deductions: No product photos of Spirit Bottles or THC Drops — back side calls out three product categories but only RTD cans are visually represented
Compliance line present in JSON but no dedicated asset slot or placement instruction for print-safe sizing
All front and back copy, including mandatory 'Legal in Missouri', is present.
All specified distributor, brand logos, lifestyle photos, and QR codes are provided.
All required assets present, but 2 logos are too sparse/low-res for print
No missing asset, but 2 logos may not be usable for premium print

fix.problem: Third lifestyle slot unused for product category coverage; Spirit Bottles and THC Drops have zero visual representation
fix.prompt_delta: Flat-lay or styled shot of a premium spirit bottle alongside small dropper/tincture bottle on a dark bar surface; warm amber lighting; hospitality-grade product photography; no people

evidence: images 14-15: 444x444px QR PNGs are program-rendered, clean black-on-white — asset_add_file and image_crop_and_resize can consume directly
All brand logos at 1024x1024px on white backgrounds — suitable for asset_add_file and placement in Express template
deductions: Several brand logos (images 3,4,5) have white backgrounds, not transparent PNGs — placement on dark (#18271F) postcard background will require background removal step not in the stated workflow
image 3 (brand_logos[1] Howdy) self-QC score 4 — raster detail at print scale may be insufficient for 5x7 at 300dpi
Some brand logos (image 3, 4, 5) have low self-QC scores, indicating potential quality issues.
All assets are in standard formats and resolutions suitable for the workflow steps.
All assets are in correct format/dims for workflow
2 logos may require minor cleanup before print

evidence: image 11 (lifestyle_photos[0]): professional bar photography, correct hospitality aesthetic, indistinguishable from real client asset
image 3 (brand_logos[1] Howdy) and image 4 (brand_logos[2] THC Social): appear AI-generated or recreated rather than supplied by actual brand owners
deductions: THC Social logo (image 4) looks like a generic SaaS chat-app icon, not a cannabis beverage brand — real client would supply actual brand files
Stay Cool logo (image 5) is a plain text wordmark that reads as a placeholder, not a real brand asset
Lifestyle photos (image 11-13) are professional and depict realistic premium hospitality scenes.
Brand logos (image 2-10) show a realistic diversity of styles for a distributor's portfolio.
Most logos/photos are realistic client handoff quality
2 logos (brand_logos[2], brand_logos[3]) look synthetic or unfinished

fix.problem: THC Social logo looks like generic tech SaaS icon, not a THC beverage brand; undermines premium hospitality feel
fix.prompt_delta: THC Social brand logo for a premium cannabis social beverage; modern sans-serif wordmark with subtle social/community motif; dark charcoal or teal palette; beverage-industry aesthetic; transparent PNG background

evidence: postcard_copy.json and distributor_logo (image 1) consistently use Arch & Prairie brand with correct palette hex colors (#18271F, #B97A36)
Brand logos span navy blue (HiSide), rope-brown (Howdy), teal (THC Social), sky blue (Stay Cool), orange (Don't Be That Dude), crimson (Tempters) — no unifying palette treatment applied
deductions: Nine partner logos have wildly divergent color palettes with no normalization strategy; on a dark #18271F background they will clash visually
No style-guide asset or placement spec showing how logos will be unified (e.g., all-white knockout treatment) — executor must guess
Distributor logo (image 1) uses brand palette and premium aesthetic.
Postcard copy (postcard_copy.json) maintains confident B2B, hospitality-grade voice.
Most assets fit premium, modern, hospitality vibe
2 logos (brand_logos[2], brand_logos[3]) break visual coherence

fix.problem: Stay Cool logo (sky blue on white) has no coherence treatment for dark background placement; no knockout/white version provided
fix.prompt_delta: Provide Stay Cool logo as white monochrome knockout version on transparent PNG background; retain original letterform and outline style; suitable for placement on dark (#18271F) background

evidence: Decision to consolidate front+back copy into single postcard_copy.json with emphasize:true flag on 'Legal in Missouri' is sound and directly serves fill_text determinism
Canva editable file correctly recorded as human-handoff item outside Adobe workflow scope — avoids false gap
deductions: No assumption recorded about logo normalization strategy (white knockout vs. full-color) for dark background — a real art director would flag this
Orientation (horizontal vs. vertical) left entirely open per brief, but no recommendation recorded despite brief explicitly inviting designer recommendation
Consolidating copy into one JSON with emphasis flag is efficient and accurate.
Output spec definition correctly separates workflow deliverables from human tasks.
All assumptions are justified and documented
No real input wrongly demoted to assumption

top_risks: CLIENT-REJECT (sev 8): Multiple brand logos (brand_logos[2] and brand_logos[3]) have vision scores of 3 — critically low quality — meaning they are likely too degraded, low-contrast, or illegible to reproduce cleanly on a premium print piece. I will not approve a handoff where partner brand logos print as muddy blobs on a piece going to bars and liquor stores.

AGENT CHOKE on 'brand_logos[2]': Vision score of 3 means the automated image_crop_and_resize step will attempt to scale an already-degraded raster logo to fit a print-resolution 5x7 layout, producing a pixelated or unreadable result that fails print QC before the document_convert_pdf step is even reached.
